title: tolerance_budget
description: Phase 2 contract; Phase 3 split. The promotion-threshold knobs the correction miner uses. Phase 3 moved mutable per-source state out of this file into the per-source diagnostic artifact `tolerance_budget_state` (one file per source under `processed/meetings/<source_id>/diagnostics/`) so the contracts file holds only immutable bounds. The bound on `current_promotion_buffer` is enforced here at write time so a malformed budget file fails closed before the miner makes a promotion decision.

schema_version.description: 1.0.0 = Phase 2 (carried `per_source_budgets` inline). 1.1.0 = Phase 3 (per-source state moved to the data lake; the property is removed from this schema). 1.2.0 = Phase 4 (adds `bootstrap_variance`, the third-tier fallback used when no source in the lake yet has enough runs to compute either a per-source or a global-median budget). All three versions remain accepted so a checkout that lags the file edit still validates.

min_promotion_buffer.description: Bound: the buffer must never drop below 2 F1 points beyond the variance budget. Fixed at the schema layer so an edit to widen the bound is auditable as a schema change, not a config tweak.

max_promotion_buffer.description: Bound: the buffer must never exceed 10 F1 points. Fixed at the schema layer.

current_promotion_buffer.description: Active buffer added on top of the variance budget. Must satisfy min_promotion_buffer <= current_promotion_buffer <= max_promotion_buffer.

global_median_budget.description: Fallback variance budget when fewer than 3 non-legacy runs have been observed for a source (read from the per-source state artifact in the data lake). Used when AT LEAST ONE source in the lake has accumulated >= 3 runs.

bootstrap_variance.description: Phase 4: third-tier fallback used by `calibration.budget.get_variance_budget` when NO source in the lake yet has enough runs to compute either a per-source or a global-median budget. Bounded [0.02, 0.15] at the schema layer so a typo cannot widen the threshold beyond the contracted range. Removed on rollback (see docs/architecture/rollback_contracts.md Phase 4 entry).
